mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2021-01-15 indicates zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. Equity investors reprice growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the signal propagates.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,631k as of 2021-01-15 reflects post-COVID recovery phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following PAYEMS declines > 0.5%, or signal fails to predict direction with Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth > 2%, aggregate demand expectations rise, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST growth > 2% AND RSAFS growth > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,669k and RSAFS at 540,915M as of 2021-01-15 reflect post-pandemic demand recovery
regime_hint: post-shock demand recovery with synchronized housing and consumption growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances when both HOUST and RSAFS grow > 2%, or interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistically significant improvement over univariate HOUST or RSAFS signals (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential margin compression for equities. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.7244 as of 2021-01-15 reflects post-pandemic manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating negative real rates. This signals monetary policy lag and future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 262.231 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2021-01-15
regime_hint: negative real rates with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1%, or interaction term shows no significant incremental predictive power beyond univariate inflation signal (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, signaling persistent labor market slack. Equity investors interpret this as reduced near-term inflation risk and sustained monetary accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.3% as of 2021-01-15 reflects post-COVID labor market recovery phase
regime_hint: elevated unemployment with slack labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 6.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized in the most recent quarter, signaling robust aggregate demand and earnings growth. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,780.325 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2020-10-15 reflects post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: above-trend GDP growth and late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances when GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) both show month-over-month growth > 1.5%, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion. This joint signal indicates robust productive capacity and employment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 1.5% AND PAYEMS growth > 1.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.7244 and PAYEMS at 142,631k as of 2021-01-15 reflect synchronized post-pandemic recovery
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor expansion in recovery phase
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow > 1.5%, or interaction effect shows no statistically significant improvement over univariate signals (p > 0.12).
